content: We can reframe curiosity-driven autonomous agents as built-in active learning memory updaters: instead of treating memory as a static storage layer for active learning, a curiosity mechanism (ICM/RND) lets the agent continuously prioritize writing high-uncertainty, information-dense new memories to its working context without explicit human annotation, turning memory itself into an active learning loop. This creates a self-sustaining system where the agent’s intrinsic curiosity drives both what it remembers and what it chooses to learn next.
